Abuse: physical abuse or mistreatment of a person
an aggressive attack or rough handling of someone
violent abusive behavior directed at another person
Arrest: law enforcement restraining and detaining a person
Arson: someone causing a fire or burning incident
Assault: a person physically attacking another person
an assaultive attack with punches kicks or force
violent aggression directed at someone nearby
Burglary: breaking into a building to steal
unauthorized entry into a property for theft
a burglary or break-in taking place
Explosion: a sudden detonation with fire and smoke
an explosive incident causing a violent burst
Fighting: mutual violence with punches kicks or grappling
Normal: ordinary everyday activity with no crime or anomaly
a normal scene without violence theft or danger
people moving or standing in a routine non-criminal situation
RoadAccidents: a traffic collision or crash on the street
vehicles crashing or colliding in traffic
Robbery: stealing from a person through force or threat
taking valuables directly from someone during a crime
Shooting: someone using a firearm against others
Shoplifting: concealing or taking merchandise without paying
a shoplifting incident inside a retail setting
Stealing: taking property that does not belong to the person
Vandalism: damaging or defacing property on purpose
an act of vandalism against objects or buildings
intentional destruction or damage of public or private property
